name: The Carter Center
mission: It seeks to prevent and resolve conflicts, enhance freedom and democracy, and improve health.
theoryOfChange: The Carter Center has a published "Peace Programs Strategic Plan" (2022-2027) which outlines its vision, goals, and objectives for preventing or mitigating the impact of war and conflict and promoting democracy, human rights, and the rule of law around the world. This strategic plan describes the world The Carter Center envisions, with countries and communities that are more peaceful, inclusive, participatory, empowered, equitable, transparent, and accountable. The plan also details new programming tools and approaches, including enhanced collaboration with local stakeholders, extensive integration and coordination across program areas for more impactful programs, and an analytical approach incorporating cross-cutting themes like gender, climate change, and health. It commits to implementing high-quality, evidence-based programs and includes a plan for creating an implementation plan to ensure that the vision, goals, and objectives can be measured and captured.
scoreSummary: The Carter Center Inc shows good impact and moderate alignment (significant risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: New York University Caregiver Intervention (NYUCI) demonstration projects

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: 87% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.

alignment.rationale: Alignment 26/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

score_details.score_summary: The Carter Center Inc shows good impact and moderate alignment (significant risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://www.cartercenter.org/ways-to-give/)

all_citations.claim: The organization reported a program expense ratio of 81.4% and fundraising efficiency of $0.07 per $1 raised.
The Carter Center establishes international normative standards for elections and operates a Conflict Resolution Program.
all_citations.source_name: Model Commitments for Advancing Genuine and Credible Elections

all_citations.claim: The organization provides various donation options for general charitable giving and sadaqah.
The Carter Center Inc reported total revenue of $162,384,287 and holds 91.0 months of working capital.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its massive operational scale and financial efficiency, balanced against a medium data confidence level regarding specific impact metrics.
areas_for_improvement: Limited specific data regarding cost-per-beneficiary metrics for health and peace programs
Moderate alignment for donors seeking high-urgency advocacy versus direct humanitarian relief

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization offers a medium fit for donors, focusing on long-term advocacy and democratic stability rather than immediate crisis relief [2]. It provides clear pathways for charitable giving through sadaqah, supporting its broad mission to improve global health and freedom [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The Center demonstrates direct impact through its Conflict Resolution Program and by setting global standards for genuine elections [2]. Financially, it maintains a robust 81.4% program expense ratio and significant working capital of 91.0 months, though specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not fully detailed in public filings [1][4].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Carter Center Inc works to advance global peace and health by resolving conflicts and protecting democratic integrity through international election observation.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 81.4%, ensuring the majority of funds reach core programs
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with only $0.07 spent per $1 raised
Strong leadership in establishing international normative standards for credible elections
baseline_narrative.summary: Founded on the mission to prevent conflict and enhance freedom, The Carter Center Inc operates major initiatives including the Conflict Resolution Program and global Election Observation [2]. The organization reported a total revenue of $162,384,287, maintaining a high level of operational liquidity with 91.0 months of working capital [1][4].
